hero.headline: Stop Scam Calls Before They Happen
hero.subhead: Escronet listens on-device, transcribes in real time, and alerts you — and a trusted guardian — the moment a call turns suspicious.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.body: All transcription and AI classification runs entirely on your device. No audio, text, or personal data ever leaves your phone.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.body: The full codebase is publicly auditable on GitHub. No black boxes, no hidden telemetry — just code you can verify yourself.

free.title: Completely Free
free.body: No subscriptions, no premium tiers, no data harvesting. Escronet is free because protecting people shouldn't have a price.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1Body: Escronet notices an incoming call from an unknown number and begins monitoring.
howItWorks.step2Body: Whisper runs locally on your phone, converting speech to text — nothing is sent to the cloud.
howItWorks.step3Body: An ONNX classifier scores the transcript. If it looks like a scam, you and your guardian are alerted immediately.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How does Escronet detect scam calls?
faq.a1: When a call comes in from an unknown number, Escronet records audio locally and runs it through an on-device Whisper model to transcribe speech in real time. The transcript is scored by an ONNX classifier trained on scam patterns. If the score crosses a risk threshold, you receive an immediate local alert.
faq.q2: Does Escronet send my call audio to a server?
faq.a2: No. All audio processing happens entirely on your device. No audio, transcripts, or personal data are ever uploaded to any server. The only data that reaches our backend is an anonymised risk event used to notify your guardian.
faq.a3: A Guardian is a trusted contact — a family member or friend — who receives a push notification whenever your device detects a high-risk call. They can monitor your alerts in real time through the Escronet app.
faq.a4: Android support is fully available now. An iOS version scoped to Guardian features (receiving alerts for people you protect) is in active development.
faq.a5: Yes, completely free. There are no subscriptions, in-app purchases, or premium tiers. We don't sell your data either.
faq.a6: Yes. The full codebase is available on GitHub. You can audit the code, open issues, or contribute.
faq.a7: On Android: phone state (to detect calls), microphone (to transcribe audio), contacts (to identify unknown callers), and SMS (to detect OTP codes sent during calls). All processing stays on-device.
faq.q8: How do I set up a Guardian?
faq.a8: Go to Settings → Guardian Setup and tap 'Add Guardian'. Share the generated QR code with your trusted contact. Once they scan it with their Escronet app, they become your guardian and will receive alerts for your calls.

phoneCheck.title: Check a Phone Number
phoneCheck.subtitle: See if a number has been reported for scam activity across our network.
phoneCheck.resultCleanSub: This number has no scam reports in our database.
phoneCheck.noAlerts: No alerts on record for this number.

install.title: Up and Running in 3 Steps
install.s1b: Install Escronet from Google Play. No email or password needed — your device is your identity.
install.s2b: Grant the permissions needed for on-device protection. Every permission is used solely for scam detection — nothing else.
install.s2link: See exactly what we request and why →
install.s3b: Escronet runs silently in the background during calls. Optionally pair a Guardian so a trusted contact is also alerted.

permissionsPage.title: App Permissions
permissionsPage.subtitle: Every permission Escronet requests has a single purpose: detecting scam calls on your device. No permission is used for advertising, tracking, or data collection.
permissionsPage.neverLeaves: All audio capture and transcription run entirely on your device. Nothing is ever uploaded.
permissionsPage.androidIntro: These permissions allow Escronet to monitor calls, analyse speech locally, and alert you and your guardian in real time.
permissionsPage.iosIntro: iOS restricts third-party access to active phone calls, so the iOS app focuses on Guardian functionality — receiving and reviewing alerts for people you protect.
permissionsPage.iosNote: Full call interception and on-device transcription require Android. If you want to be protected (not just act as a guardian), an Android device is needed.

android.p1why: Detects when an incoming call starts from an unknown number so Escronet can begin monitoring.
android.p1privacy: No call content is read. Only the call event (started / ended) is observed.
android.p2why: Captures in-call audio to feed into the on-device Whisper transcription model in real time.
android.p2privacy: Audio is processed on your device and immediately discarded. It is never stored, transmitted, or shared.
android.p3why: Distinguishes calls from your saved contacts (trusted) versus unknown numbers (monitored for scams).
android.p3privacy: Your contact list is read locally only. It is never uploaded to any server.
android.p4why: Detects OTP codes sent by scammers during a call so a warning banner appears before you read them aloud.
android.p4privacy: Only messages received during an active monitored call are inspected. No SMS content is stored or forwarded.
android.p5why: Delivers local scam alerts on your screen and push notifications to your guardian when risk is detected.
android.p5privacy: Guardian alerts contain only a risk level and timestamp — no audio, no transcript, no personal data.
android.p6why: Keeps the protection service alive during a call so the OS cannot suspend it mid-analysis.
android.p6privacy: The service runs only while a call is active. It does not run continuously in the background.

ios.p1why: Delivers alerts to your device when someone you are guarding receives a suspicious call.
ios.p1privacy: Notifications contain only a risk level and timestamp — no audio, no transcript.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Your Account
deleteAccount.subtitle: This will permanently remove all your data, alerts, and guardian links from Escronet. This action cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.userIdPlaceholder: Paste your User ID from the app
deleteAccount.userIdHint: Find your User ID in the app under Settings → Device ID.
deleteAccount.confirmLabel: I understand this is permanent and cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.errorFormat: User ID must be a valid UUID.
deleteAccount.errorNotFound: No account found with that User ID.
deleteAccount.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

deleteAccountSuccess.title: Account Deleted
deleteAccountSuccess.body: Your account and all associated data have been permanently removed from Escronet.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.intro: Escronet is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights as a user.
privacyPage.s1b: When you register, we generate an anonymous device identifier and collect a Firebase device token to send push notifications. No email address or password is required. Caller phone numbers are synchronised to your device to identify unknown callers. If you pair with a guardian, we store the association between your account and theirs. When a call is classified as suspicious, we store an anonymised risk event (risk level and timestamp only) to notify your guardian.
privacyPage.s2b: We do not collect, store, or transmit your call audio. We do not collect or store call transcripts. All audio capture, Whisper transcription, and ONNX classification run entirely on your device and never leave it.
privacyPage.s3b: We use your data solely to provide the service: sending push notifications to you and your guardian when a suspicious call is detected, synchronising caller reputation data to your device, and maintaining guardian–user relationships.
privacyPage.s4b: We do not sell your data to any third party. Risk event notifications are shared with your guardian only if you have explicitly paired with one. We use Google Firebase for push notifications; Firebase processes device tokens according to Google's Privacy Policy.
privacyPage.s5b: Alert data is retained for 12 months from creation. Account and guardian relationship data is retained until you delete your account. You may request deletion of all your data at any time by contacting us.
privacyPage.s6b: If you are located in the European Union, you have the right to access, correct, delete, or export your personal data; to restrict or object to processing; and to lodge a complaint with your national data protection authority. To exercise any of these rights, contact us at the address below.
privacyPage.s7b: The app requests the following permissions: Phone State — to detect incoming calls; Microphone — to transcribe audio locally (no audio leaves your device); Contacts — to identify unknown callers; SMS — to detect OTP codes sent during calls; Notifications — to alert you and your guardian.
privacyPage.s8b: All data in transit between the app and our servers is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. We follow industry-standard practices to secure stored data.
privacyPage.s9b: For privacy questions or data deletion requests, contact us at: privacy@escronet.com
